title: Pretty Egyptian White Cornmeal Pilaf
description: A quick and easy Egyptian dish featuring white cornmeal and katsuramuki-cut vegetables, perfect for a weeknight meal. This jain-friendly recipe is made with fresh and healthy ingredients.
instructions: Rinse the white cornmeal in a fine mesh sieve and drain well.
In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil.
Add the white cornmeal to the saucepan and reduce the heat to low.
Cover the saucepan with a lid and simmer for 10 minutes.
While the cornmeal is cooking, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the katsuramuki-cut carrots and zucchini to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the chopped green onions to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Fluff the cooked cornmeal with a fork and stir in the lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
To serve, divide the cornmeal pilaf among individual bamboo trays and top with the cooked vegetables.
